title: Intensely Savory Norwegian Herring Fried Rice
description: A cultured and chewy Norwegian dish featuring herring, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with low sodium ingredients and cooked in an iron teppan.
instructions: Heat the iron teppan over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the grated carrot and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Push the onion and carrot mixture to one side of the teppan.
Add a little more oil if necessary, then add the beaten eggs and scramble them until they are cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the onion and carrot mixture.
Add the cooked rice to the teppan and stir-fry for about 5 minutes, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Add the pickled herring fillets, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper, and stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the Norwegian herring fried rice hot.
